watch: Watch how a tiny 10,000-capacity stadium and shoestring budget try to survive against Bundesliga giants — Elversberg's rapid four-division climb is one of German football's purest underdog stories.
lastSeason: Won automatic promotion from the 2. Bundesliga on the final day of 2025-26 to reach the Bundesliga for the first time in the club's history.

name: SC Paderborn 07
identity: East Westphalia's high-wire overachiever — a small-budget club that has twice bounced into the Bundesliga on the back of a relentless, high-risk pressing style, only to be relegated immediately both times.
history: SC Paderborn 07 spent most of its history in the German lower leagues before a meteoric rise under manager André Breitenreiter delivered a first-ever promotion to the Bundesliga in 2014. That debut season produced the Bundesliga's longest-ever recorded goal, an 83-metre volley by Moritz Stoppelkamp, but a second-half collapse saw the newcomers relegated on the final day. Paderborn returned to the Bundesliga for the 2019-20 season, again playing an aggressive, high-press system, but finished bottom of the table and went straight back down. The club has built a reputation as one of the Bundesliga's classic small-budget disruptors, using intense pressing and tactical daring to punch above its financial weight even when the results don't hold. Paderborn returned to the top flight once more for 2026-27, aiming to finally break its immediate-relegation pattern.
rivals: 1. FC Köln — a regional North Rhine-Westphalia rivalry
Borussia Mönchengladbach — a regional rivalry
Borussia Dortmund — a regional rivalry lacking a formal derby history
watch: Watch for chaotic, end-to-end matches — Paderborn's high-press, high-risk style produces some of the most entertaining, error-prone football in the league, for better or worse.
lastSeason: Won promotion to the Bundesliga via the relegation/promotion play-off in 2025-26 (beating Wolfsburg), returning to the top flight after six years away.

name: FC Lorient
identity: Brittany's model development club — a quietly-run academy factory that has spent decades turning young talent into transfer profit while yo-yoing between France's top two divisions.
history: Lorient spent most of its early existence as a minor amateur outfit before a 1980s revival under manager Christian Gourcuff, whose methodical coaching philosophy became the template for the club's identity. The Merlus reached their high-water mark in 2002, beating Bastia 1-0 in the Coupe de France final for the club's only major trophy, then backed it up with European football the following season. Since then Lorient has built a reputation as a reliable Ligue 2/Ligue 1 shuttle side, repeatedly promoted on the strength of academy graduates and smart sporting-director recruitment rather than spending power. Christian's son Yoann Gourcuff came through the Lorient academy before becoming a French international, epitomizing the club's development-first ethos, and strikers like Kevin Gameiro built their reputations at the Moustoir before bigger moves.
rivals: Rennes — the primary Breton derby and Lorient's biggest rivalry
Guingamp — a smaller, more regional Breton fixture
Brest — another Brittany meeting, more geographic than heated
watch: Watch for the academy pipeline — Lorient is consistently a place where young French talent gets its first real minutes before moving on to bigger clubs.
lastSeason: Retained their Ligue 1 place in 2025-26, finishing 10th on their return to the top flight.

name: Paris FC
identity: The other Paris club — a long-suffering, historically overshadowed second team from the capital that has been transformed almost overnight by Arnault family and Red Bull investment into PSG's first true intracity rival in decades.
history: Formed in 1969, Paris FC briefly merged with Stade Saint-Germain to create Paris Saint-Germain in 1970 before splitting off again in 1972 to go its own way, leaving PSG to become the capital's dominant club. For over four decades Paris FC drifted through Ligue 2 and the third tier, playing in front of sparse crowds at the athletics-track Stade Charléty, a stadium ill-suited to atmosphere. Everything changed in late 2024 when the Arnault family (LVMH) took a majority stake alongside a minority investment from Red Bull, pouring in resources and footballing expertise led by figures like Jürgen Klopp. The revamped club moved to Stade Jean-Bouin and won promotion to Ligue 1 in May 2025, ending a 46-year absence from the top flight and setting up Paris's first derby with PSG in over three decades.
rivals: Paris Saint-Germain — the emerging Paris derby, freighted with a David-vs-Goliath, old-money-vs-new-money storyline
watch: Watch the Paris derby narrative — a fraction of PSG's budget and history, but backed by fresh billionaire money and positioning itself as the identity-driven counterweight to PSG's galactico model.
lastSeason: Impressed on their top-flight debut season in 2025-26, finishing 11th in Ligue 1.

name: ES Troyes AC
identity: A small Champagne-region club whose fortunes have swung between surprise European nights and yo-yo relegations, now steered by City Football Group's global scouting network.
history: Formed in 1986 out of the financial wreckage of an earlier Troyes club, ESTAC's best era came in the early 2000s under president-driven ambition that carried them to seventh-place Ligue 1 finishes and a memorable 2001 Intertoto Cup campaign, including a famous win over Newcastle United. The club has since become one of French football's classic elevator teams, bouncing between Ligue 1 and Ligue 2 through the 2010s and 2020s. In 2020, City Football Group took ownership, plugging Troyes into the same data-driven recruitment network as Manchester City, using the Aube club partly as a proving ground for young talent across the group's global system. Troyes returned to Ligue 1 for 2026-27 after topping Ligue 2, capping a title-winning campaign that included a statement win away at Saint-Étienne.
rivals: Stade de Reims — the closest regional Champagne/Marne rivalry
AS Saint-Étienne — a fixture sharpened by their 2025-26 promotion race
watch: Watch for City Football Group fingerprints — a possession-based, recruitment-driven identity layered onto a historically modest, yo-yo Champagne club.
lastSeason: Won the 2025-26 Ligue 2 title (67 points) to earn automatic promotion to Ligue 1.

name: Le Mans FC
identity: A Sarthe club overshadowed globally by its city's famous 24-hour endurance race, whose own football story is one of near-death financial collapse followed by an astonishing climb back through six tiers of French football.
history: Formed in 1985 as Le Mans Union Club 72, the club enjoyed its best spell in Ligue 1 between 2003 and 2010, fielding sides featuring Grafite, Romaric and a young Gervinho and reaching as high as ninth place. Financial mismanagement caught up with the club after relegation, and by 2013 Le Mans FC had gone bankrupt and been administratively demoted all the way to regional amateur football, a fall of six divisions. What followed was a remarkable rebuild: consecutive promotions clawed the club back up through the pyramid, culminating in back-to-back promotions from the third tier in 2024-25 and from Ligue 2 in 2025-26. The 2026-27 return to Ligue 1 comes seventeen years after the club was last in the top flight, playing out of the MMArena inside the Circuit de la Sarthe, better known worldwide for hosting the 24 Hours of Le Mans.
rivals: Stade Lavallois — the Derby du Maine, contested across the Sarthe-Mayenne boundary
Angers — a broader Pays de la Loire regional rivalry
watch: Watch for the comeback narrative — a club that fell to amateur regional football in 2013 and clawed its way back to Ligue 1 through six divisions in just over a decade.
lastSeason: Finished 2nd in the 2025-26 Ligue 2 — losing only four league games all season — to win promotion to Ligue 1.

name: Club Atlante
nick: Los Potros de Hierro, La Potranca
honours: 3 Liga MX titles (1940s dynasty era, Apertura 2007), 1 Campeón de Campeones, 2 Copa México titles, 2 CONCACAF Champions' Cup (1983, 2008-09)
identity: Mexico City's original working-class club and one of the country's oldest institutions, Atlante built its identity as "El Equipo del Pueblo" in direct contrast to the capital's establishment sides, and its current return to the top flight via a purchased franchise slot has only added to its reputation as Liga MX's most improbable survivor.
history: Founded on April 18, 1916, under the name Sinaloa before passing through incarnations as Lusitania, U-53, and Atlántico, the club settled on Atlante in 1921 and quickly became a Mexico City institution known for its resilient, iron-willed sides. Atlante won Mexican titles across the 1940s golden era, added continental glory with CONCACAF Champions' Cup wins in 1983 and 2008-09, and famously lifted an Apertura 2007 title just months after relocating to Cancún. Financial collapse and administrative turmoil pushed the club out of Liga MX entirely in 2020, and Atlante spent over a decade in the second-tier Liga de Expansión MX while its historic identity was kept alive largely through loyalist fans and its storied rivalry with América. In a move unprecedented in Liga MX history, Atlante secured its return to the top flight for 2026-27 not through sporting promotion but by purchasing the Primera División franchise membership of Mazatlán FC, which was dissolved, allowing Atlante to relocate the slot back to Mexico City and resume life at Estadio Banorte alongside Cruz Azul and América. The deal is a stark reminder that Liga MX's playoff-based promotion system was only just reinstated by a CAS ruling, and that a club's place in the top flight can still be bought as easily as it can be earned.
rivals: Club América — the Clásico Capitalino, one of Mexico City's oldest and most historic derbies, rooted in Atlante's working-class identity against América's establishment image
Cruz Azul — a modern Mexico City rivalry sharpened by Atlante's return to groundshare at Estadio Banorte
Pumas UNAM — a historic capital rivalry, including Atlante's 2007 Apertura final win over Pumas
watch: Watch for the emotional weight of Atlante's first Clásico Capitalino against América back in Mexico City after more than a decade away, and notice how a fanbase that survived relegation, relocation, and years in the second division reacts to reclaiming a seat at Liga MX's top table by checkbook rather than by winning promotion on the pitch.
lastSeason: Returned to Liga MX for the Apertura 2026 tournament after 12 years in the second tier, acquiring the top-flight franchise formerly held by Mazatlán FC.
